scenario: Customer requested a refund for a laptop delivered 33 days ago, outside the 30-day window.
Customer requested a refund for wireless earbuds that had been opened and used for a week.
Customer requested a third refund within the same 12-month period after two prior refunds.
VIP customer requested a refund 35 days after delivery; annual manual override was still available and was applied.
Customer requested a refund on an order totalling ₹72,000 for a home theatre system.
Customer requested a refund for a software license key that had already been delivered by email.
Customer reported a ceramic dinner set arrived shattered; photos confirmed damage on arrival, refund issued within window.
Account with a high fraud risk score requested a refund claiming the parcel never arrived despite delivery confirmation.
Customer returned an unopened smartphone in original sealed packaging 12 days after delivery.
Customer requested a refund for a t-shirt that did not fit, 20 days after delivery, first refund of the year.
VIP customer requested an out-of-window refund but had already used their annual override in March.
Customer requested a refund for a gift card that had been partially spent.
